title: Elon Musk’s SpaceX and xAI Are Planning a Megamerger of Rockets and AI
title_cn: 埃隆·马斯克的SpaceX和xAI正计划进行火箭与人工智能的超级合并

paragraphs.en: Updated Jan. 31, 2026 8:26 am ET
paragraphs.cn: 更新于2026年1月31日上午8:26（美国东部时间）

paragraphs.en: Elon Musk’s rocket-maker SpaceX and artificial-intelligence startup xAI are planning to merge into one company, people familiar with the matter said, further consolidating the billionaire’s business empire.
paragraphs.cn: 知情人士透露，埃隆·马斯克旗下的火箭制造商SpaceX和人工智能初创公司xAI正计划合并为一家公司，此举将进一步巩固这位亿万富翁的商业帝国。

paragraphs.en: SpaceX executives have started to tell some investors about the planned tie-up, one of the people said.
paragraphs.cn: 其中一位知情人士表示，SpaceX高管已开始向部分投资者透露这项计划中的合作。

paragraphs.en: It is possible that a merger won’t happen and talks could fall apart or that Musk could decide on a different path.
paragraphs.cn: 合并有可能不会发生，谈判可能会破裂，或者马斯克可能会决定采取不同的路径。

paragraphs.en: Both companies are privately held, and the math of a potential deal wasn’t immediately clear. xAI sought to raise $15 billion from investors for a $230 billion valuation last year, while SpaceX was seeking an $800 billion valuation in a December tender offer, The Wall Street Journal previously reported.
paragraphs.cn: 两家公司均为私人控股，潜在交易的财务细节尚不明确。《华尔街日报》此前报道，xAI去年寻求从投资者那里筹集150亿美元，估值达到2300亿美元；而SpaceX在去年12月的一次要约收购中寻求8000亿美元的估值。

paragraphs.en: Reuters reported earlier that SpaceX and xAI were in discussions to combine.
paragraphs.cn: 路透社早些时候报道称，SpaceX和xAI正在讨论合并事宜。

paragraphs.en: Musk has launched plans to take SpaceX public this year in what could be one of the biggest initial public offerings on record. It also wasn’t clear how the rocket maker’s offering would be affected by merging with an artificial-intelligence startup and a social-media company.
paragraphs.cn: 马斯克已启动计划，拟于今年将SpaceX上市，这可能成为有史以来规模最大的首次公开募股之一。目前尚不清楚这家火箭制造商的上市计划将如何受到与一家人工智能初创公司和一家社交媒体公司合并的影响。

paragraphs.en: The Journal reported that Musk had decided to take SpaceX public in part to raise more capital to build data centers in space and to help xAI. And investors have long speculated Musk could combine more of his sprawling businesses, which also include Tesla, such as how he merged xAI and X last year.
paragraphs.cn: 《华尔街日报》报道称，马斯克决定将SpaceX上市，部分原因是为了筹集更多资金，用于在太空建造数据中心并帮助xAI。投资者长期以来一直猜测马斯克可能会整合他旗下更多庞大的业务，其中包括特斯拉，就像他去年合并xAI和X一样。

paragraphs.en: Operating data centers in space is an unproven concept that has grabbed the attention of executives like Musk, Jeff Bezos and Sam Altman.
paragraphs.cn: 在太空运营数据中心是一个未经证实的理念，但已引起了马斯克、杰夫·贝佐斯和萨姆·奥尔特曼等高管的关注。

paragraphs.en: Musk has previously mused about a broader tie-up of his companies. Several years ago, he described the idea of forming a holding company for Tesla, SpaceX, Neuralink and Boring Co. as a “good idea.”
paragraphs.cn: 马斯克此前曾构想过他旗下公司进行更广泛的整合。几年前，他将为特斯拉、SpaceX、Neuralink和Boring Co.组建一家控股公司的想法描述为“一个好主意”。

paragraphs.en: Inside his empire, companies regularly share resources, including employees. SpaceX previously invested $2 billion in xAI, and on Wednesday, Tesla disclosed its own $2 billion investment in the most recent funding round for xAI.
paragraphs.cn: 在他的商业帝国中，各公司定期共享资源，包括员工。SpaceX此前向xAI投资了20亿美元，而周三，特斯拉披露其在xAI最新一轮融资中也投资了20亿美元。

paragraphs.en: SpaceX is a crown jewel of Musk’s businesses, advancing space technology and frequently handling high-profile missions. It is one of the highest-valued private companies in the world and recently interviewed banks for a summer IPO with aspirations to raise billions of dollars, the Journal reported.
paragraphs.cn: SpaceX是马斯克旗下业务的“皇冠上的明珠”，它推动了空间技术的发展，并经常处理备受瞩目的任务。《华尔街日报》报道称，它是全球估值最高的私营公司之一，最近还与银行进行了夏季IPO的洽谈，目标是筹集数十亿美元。

paragraphs.en: xAI, meanwhile, has struggled to attract as many enterprise customers and individual users as competitors like OpenAI and Anthropic. It has announced some partnerships with government customers, including the Pentagon, which SpaceX also does business with.
paragraphs.cn: 与此同时，xAI在吸引企业客户和个人用户方面，不及OpenAI和Anthropic等竞争对手。它已宣布与包括五角大楼在内的一些政府客户建立合作关系，而SpaceX也与五角大楼有业务往来。

paragraphs.en: The artificial-intelligence company has also found itself embroiled in a number of controversies, most recently including the dissemination of sexualized images of users produced without their consent.
paragraphs.cn: 这家人工智能公司也卷入了多起争议，最近的一次是未经用户同意传播了生成的用户性化图片。

paragraphs.en: At the World Economic Forum in Davos, Musk said last week that he believes the future of artificial-intelligence data centers is out in the stars, where they can be powered by the sun. His data centers around the Memphis area have had to contend with energy constraints and Musk brought in a number of portable gas turbines to power the facilities.
paragraphs.cn: 在达沃斯世界经济论坛上，马斯克上周表示，他相信人工智能数据中心的未来在太空中，在那里它们可以由太阳能供电。他在孟菲斯地区的数据中心不得不应对能源限制，马斯克为此引入了多台便携式燃气轮机为这些设施供电。

paragraphs.en: “The lowest-cost place to put AI will be space,” Musk said on a panel. “That will be true within two years, maybe three.”
paragraphs.cn: 马斯克在一个小组讨论中说：“放置人工智能成本最低的地方将是太空。这将在两年内，也许三年内成为现实。”

paragraphs.en: On Friday, SpaceX said it wants to develop a network of orbital AI data centers consisting of up to one million satellites, according to filings at the Federal Communications Commission. The company will need to secure permission from the telecom regulator to deploy that fleet, addressing spectrum usage, debris risk and other issues.
paragraphs.cn: 根据提交给联邦通信委员会的文件，SpaceX周五表示，它希望开发一个由多达一百万颗卫星组成的轨道人工智能数据中心网络。该公司将需要获得电信监管机构的许可才能部署该卫星群，并解决频谱使用、碎片风险及其他问题。

paragraphs.en: Appeared in the January 31, 2026, print edition as 'Musk’s SpaceX And xAI Look to Combine'.
paragraphs.cn: 刊登于2026年1月31日印刷版，标题为“马斯克的SpaceX和xAI寻求合并”。

paragraphs.en: Alexander Saeedy covers banking and finance for The Wall Street Journal. Previously, he covered financially distressed companies and bankruptcy.
paragraphs.cn: 亚历山大·萨伊迪（Alexander Saeedy）是《华尔街日报》的银行和金融记者。此前，他曾报道财务困境公司和破产事宜。

paragraphs.en: On the banking beat, he has investigated the inner workings of the biggest companies on Wall Street. He has regularly broken scoops on how banks have raised billions of dollars for high-profile clients like Elon Musk and reported in detail on legal scandals that have roiled some of America’s most well-known financial firms. His reporting on the death of a young investment banker at Bank of America in 2024 has won multiple awards, including a New York Press Club Award and a Best in Business Award from the Society for Advancing Business Editing and Writing.
paragraphs.cn: 在银行报道领域，他调查了华尔街最大公司的内部运作。他经常独家披露银行如何为埃隆·马斯克等知名客户筹集数十亿美元，并详细报道了震动美国一些知名金融公司的法律丑闻。他关于2024年美国银行一名年轻投资银行家死亡的报道赢得了多个奖项，包括纽约新闻俱乐部奖和商业编辑与写作促进协会颁发的最佳商业奖。

paragraphs.en: Previously at the Journal, he covered financial distress and bankruptcy. His stories led him to investigate collapsed cryptocurrency companies, bankrupt opioid manufacturers and emerging-market economies unable to pay for imports of food and fuel. His coverage of Sri Lanka's financial crisis was part of a collection of stories about China's Belt & Road initiative that received an honorable mention award from the Society of Publishers in Asia in 2023.
paragraphs.cn: 此前在《华尔街日报》，他报道财务困境和破产。他的报道促使他调查了倒闭的加密货币公司、破产的阿片类药物制造商以及无力支付食品和燃料进口费用的新兴市场经济体。他对斯里兰卡金融危机的报道是关于中国“一带一路”倡议系列报道的一部分，该系列报道于2023年获得了亚洲出版业协会的荣誉奖。

paragraphs.en: At the start of his career, he worked for Reuters News in Brussels, where he covered the end of the eurozone debt crisis and the Brexit referendum. He later worked as a freelancer in Brussels for two years, covering European economic and political stories for the Atlantic, Foreign Affairs, Vice News, the Nation and other media.
paragraphs.cn: 职业生涯初期，他在布鲁塞尔为路透社工作，报道了欧元区债务危机的结束和英国脱欧公投。后来，他在布鲁塞尔做了两年自由撰稿人，为《大西洋月刊》、《外交事务》、《Vice新闻》、《国家》等媒体报道欧洲经济和政治新闻。

paragraphs.en: Before joining the Journal, he covered distressed debt, leveraged finance and real estate in New York for Reorg Research and S&P's LCD. He is a graduate of Yale University, where he received a bachelor's and master's degree in History.
paragraphs.cn: 在加入《华尔街日报》之前，他在纽约为Reorg Research和标普的LCD报道不良债务、杠杆融资和房地产。他毕业于耶鲁大学，获得历史学学士和硕士学位。

paragraphs.en: Berber Jin covers startups and venture capital out of the Wall Street Journal's San Francisco office. His articles focus on the money and people powering Silicon Valley, with a recent focus on artificial intelligence. He previously covered the same topic for the Information, where he won a Best in Business award from the Society for Advancing Business Editing and Writing.
paragraphs.cn: 伯伯·金（Berber Jin）在《华尔街日报》旧金山办事处报道初创公司和风险投资。他的文章关注推动硅谷发展的资金和人才，近期重点关注人工智能。他此前在The Information报道相同主题，并获得了商业编辑与写作促进协会颁发的最佳商业奖。

paragraphs.en: Berber is originally from Scarsdale, N.Y., and graduated from Stanford University.
paragraphs.cn: 伯伯·金原籍纽约斯卡斯代尔，毕业于斯坦福大学。

vocabulary.meaning_en: combining a number of things into a single more effective or coherent whole.
vocabulary.example: Elon Musk’s rocket-maker SpaceX and artificial-intelligence startup xAI are planning to merge into one company, people familiar with the matter said, further consolidating the billionaire’s business empire.
vocabulary.example_cn: 知情人士透露，埃隆·马斯克旗下的火箭制造商SpaceX和人工智能初创公司xAI正计划合并为一家公司，此举将进一步巩固这位亿万富翁的商业帝国。

vocabulary.example: Elon Musk’s rocket-maker SpaceX and artificial-intelligence startup xAI are planning to merge into one company.
vocabulary.example_cn: 埃隆·马斯克旗下的火箭制造商SpaceX和人工智能初创公司xAI正计划合并为一家公司。

vocabulary.meaning_en: a person with senior managerial responsibility in a business organization.
vocabulary.example: SpaceX executives have started to tell some investors about the planned tie-up.
vocabulary.example_cn: SpaceX高管已开始向部分投资者透露这项计划中的合作。

vocabulary.meaning_en: (of a company) owned by private individuals or investors, rather than by the public through shares traded on a stock exchange.
vocabulary.example: Both companies are privately held, and the math of a potential deal wasn’t immediately clear.
vocabulary.example_cn: 两家公司均为私人控股，潜在交易的财务细节尚不明确。

vocabulary.meaning_en: an estimation of something's worth, especially one carried out by a professional valuer.
vocabulary.example: xAI sought to raise $15 billion from investors for a $230 billion valuation last year.
vocabulary.example_cn: xAI去年寻求从投资者那里筹集150亿美元，估值达到2300亿美元。

vocabulary.meaning_en: the first sale of stock by a private company to the public.
vocabulary.example: Musk has launched plans to take SpaceX public this year in what could be one of the biggest initial public offerings on record.
vocabulary.example_cn: 马斯克已启动计划，拟于今年将SpaceX上市，这可能成为有史以来规模最大的首次公开募股之一。

vocabulary.meaning_en: spreading out over a large area in an untidy or irregular way.
vocabulary.example: And investors have long speculated Musk could combine more of his sprawling businesses, which also include Tesla.
vocabulary.example_cn: 投资者长期以来一直猜测马斯克可能会整合他旗下更多庞大的业务，其中包括特斯拉。

vocabulary.meaning_en: the most valuable or important part of something.
vocabulary.meaning_cn: 皇冠上的明珠；最珍贵的部分
vocabulary.example: SpaceX is a crown jewel of Musk’s businesses, advancing space technology and frequently handling high-profile missions.
vocabulary.example_cn: SpaceX是马斯克旗下业务的“皇冠上的明珠”，它推动了空间技术的发展，并经常处理备受瞩目的任务。

vocabulary.meaning_en: prolonged public disagreement or heated discussion.
vocabulary.example: The artificial-intelligence company has also found itself embroiled in a number of controversies.
vocabulary.example_cn: 这家人工智能公司也卷入了多起争议。

vocabulary.meaning_en: the act of spreading something, especially information, widely.
vocabulary.example: most recently including the dissemination of sexualized images of users produced without their consent.
vocabulary.example_cn: 最近的一次是未经用户同意传播了生成的用户性化图片的传播。
